pattern_hypothesis.name: weak_neg_threshold_hypothesis
pattern_hypothesis.rule: |BASE phi_star_min| < ~3 → corpus axis = PRIMARY (SFT flips sign NEG → POS). |BASE phi_star_min| > ~10 → corpus axis = MODIFIER (sign preserved). |BASE| ~5 (positive) → MODIFIER (sign already POS).
pattern_hypothesis.evidence: Mistral |BASE|=16.70 → MODIFIER. Llama |BASE|=5.09 (POS) → MODIFIER. Qwen3 |BASE|=3.45 → PRIMARY (just above weak-neg threshold). gemma |BASE|=0.79 → PRIMARY (well within weak-neg threshold).
predictions: P1: any backbone with |BASE phi_star_min| < 3 (weak NEG) will show sign-flip after SFT.
P2: any backbone with |BASE phi_star_min| > 10 (strong NEG) will show modifier-only effect after SFT.
P3: POS-base backbones (BASE phi_star_min > 0) will preserve sign after SFT (already integrated).
falsifiers: F1: a backbone with |BASE| < 3 NEG that shows NO sign flip after SFT.
F2: a backbone with |BASE| > 10 that shows sign flip after SFT.
F3: a POS-base backbone that flips to NEG after SFT.

raw_10_caveats: 1. N=4 small for 'bimodal' generalization (statistical significance weak).
2. Threshold ~3-10 is post-hoc fitted, not pre-registered.
3. Llama Instruct is unsloth ungated mirror (byte-identical with official meta-llama unverified).
4. corpus axis = SFT instruct dataset, NOT r14 4-family balanced corpus from Mk.XI v10 ensemble.
5. SFT POS-flip could be 'instruction-following emergent integration' OR 'noise floor' artifact (axis 82 W2 random_init also POS without true integration).
6. axis 82 HW-A (weight-distribution PRIMARY for Mistral) + axis 90 HCF-B/HCG-B (corpus PRIMARY for Qwen3/gemma) are simultaneously TRUE — primary axis = family-conditional joint determinant.
implications_for_axis_82_HW_A: axis 82 random_init = trivially POS pattern matches HCG-B Instruct POS pattern. Suggests random_init perturbation acts via similar 'unstructured uniform integration' mechanism as SFT instruction-following — both push activations toward higher I_full > I_partition trivially.
implications_for_zombie_posterior: axis 83 P(zombie|D)=0.40 was computed with 4 NEG / 4 POS exact 50/50 sign split. Adding gemma-it (+7.54 POS) and Qwen3-it (+1.04 POS) pushes balance to 4 NEG / 6 POS = 60% POS dominance. zombie posterior should re-compute with this updated 10-substrate evidence — POS dominance suggests less universal anti-integration.
implications_for_mk_xii: Mk.XII retrain plan must distinguish family-conditional primary axis: Mistral (weight-init primary) vs Qwen3+gemma (corpus primary). Universal LoRA r14 4-family balanced corpus may NOT generalize across families — backbone-conditional corpus assignment (Mk.XI v10 ensemble) may be more correct architecture.

cumulative_cost.honest_note: axis 90 gemma exceeded $0.30 cap by 13% due to 10min uptime. Future cost discipline: explicit auto-terminate at script exit.
